creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
[Note: The below is a personal view. I'm not a FreeBSD team member or anything. It is just what I've observed in the past.]

My understanding is that once 11.0 progressed far enough and until sometime after release/11.0.0 is official, there are no stable/11 snapshot builds done. For example during the releng/11.0 builds time frame there are no stable/11 builds made. Later the stable/11 builds start up again.

[It likely would be too much of a load on people and build machines to have two 11 variants (releng/11.0 and stable/11) having builds over that time frame, along with the normal 10 and 12 builds.]

The procedure deleting old stable/11 snapshots continues during this time so eventually there are no old stable/11 snapshots around but release/11.0.0 need not be in place yet when that happens.

One has to follow the progression over time:

pub/FreeBSD/snapshots/
then. . .
pub/FreeBSD/releases/
then. . .
pub/FreeBSD/snapshots/

If one is using pre-built materials and needs such middle-time-frame pre-built updates (or first-time materials). The "releases" ones need not have everything that stable/11 source code has. To track stable/11 for the middle time frame one must work from stable/11 source code for that middle time frame.

It was like this for 10.0 as I understand/remember and likely will be like this for 12 when it transitions from CURRENT (head) to later stages. The structure is not specific to 11.

I do not remember finding any documentation about this aspect of the transition from head (CURRENT) to stable, releng, and release for folks that normally track stable but not via source code based builds.

Getting to release/11.x.y for 0.0 < x.y likely has the same transition structure each time.
